name: Low Fat Raspberry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. In a large bowl, combine flour and brown sugar; mix well.
3. Using a fork or pastry blender, cut in margarine until coarse crumbs form.
4. Stir in walnuts. Lightly press the mixture into an ungreased 15x10x1-inch baking pan.
5. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until light golden brown. Cool.
6. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and vanilla extract until light and fluffy.
7. Add marshmallow fluff; beat just until combined.
8. Fold in the whipped topping. Refrigerate about one hour or until firm.
9. Sprinkle raspberries over the cheesecake filling.
10. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar, cornstarch, and water; mix well.
11. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture thickens and becomes clear.
12. Remove from heat; stir in gelatin until dissolved.
13. Cool glaze for 20 to 30 minutes or until lukewarm.
14. Carefully spoon glaze over the raspberries.
15. Refrigerate about one hour until firm.
16. Cut into squares. Store in refrigerator. Makes 25 servings.
Nutrient data per serving: 259 calories; 3 g protein; 40 g carbohydrates; 10 g fat; 8 mg cholesterol; 136 mg sodium.
